articles.content: Secret scanning plays a critical role in protecting developers and organizations. It helps catch exposed credentials early and prevents small mistakes from turning into real incidents. At GitHub’s scale, even small inefficiencies create real friction. Too many false positives make alerts harder to trust. When alerts feel noisy, developers spend more time triaging and less time fixing real issues. The agent is resourceful, but without a language server, that’s the best it can do.
